channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Thousands of seafarers stranded by ongoing U.S. blockade on Strait of Hormuz
item.description: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S. blockade on Iranian ports drags on, thousands of seafarers are stranded on ships, and economic shockwaves ripple around the world.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Former National Security Advisor John Bolton on Trump's handling of the Iran war
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks former national security adviser and U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations John Bolton about the potential costs of extending the ceasefire with Iran.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks former national security adviser and U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations John Bolton about the potential costs of extending the ceasefire with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: At a veteran-led protest on Capitol Hill, grief and dismay reverberate over Iran war
item.description: Photos and videos from the protest, which resulted in 66 arrests, have spread widely across social media — amplified by others who share a similar frustration and unease about the country's military action.
item.content:encoded:
Photos and videos from the protest, which resulted in 66 arrests, have spread widely across social media — amplified by others who share a similar frustration and unease about the country's military action.

(Image credit: Leigh Vogel)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Why Trump wants to spend $1 billion on Great Salt Lake
item.description: Utah's Great Salt Lake has been labeled an "environmental nuclear bomb" and it has the attention of the president of the United States.
item.content:encoded:
Utah's Great Salt Lake has been labeled an "environmental nuclear bomb" and it has the attention of the president of the United States.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Ongoing U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz strands thousands of seafarers, Trump administration eases rules on medical marijuana, Wildfires fueled by drought continue to spread in parts of Georgia.
item.content:encoded: Ongoing U.S. blockade of Strait of Hormuz strands thousands of seafarers, Trump administration eases rules on medical marijuana, Wildfires fueled by drought continue to spread in parts of Georgia.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: 'Michael' chronicles the rise of the pop superstar, avoiding the controversies
item.description: The biopic "Michael" charts the rise of Michael Jackson. But the movie might be more notable for what it leaves out of the Michael Jackson story.
item.content:encoded: The biopic "Michael" charts the rise of Michael Jackson. But the movie might be more notable for what it leaves out of the Michael Jackson story.
item.dc:creator: Aisha Harris

item.title: The compass that helped pave the way for westward expansion in the US
item.description: A compass used by Lewis and Clark on their expedition to explore the American West is owned by the Smithsonian and is part of NPR's 250th anniversary series, America In Pursuit.
item.content:encoded: A compass used by Lewis and Clark on their expedition to explore the American West is owned by the Smithsonian and is part of NPR's 250th anniversary series, America In Pursuit.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Limbong

item.title: DHS starts deportation of woman whose loved one died in the Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.
item.content:encoded:
The mother's story is a stark example of how immigration policy can change drastically with each administration — and transform the lives of immigrants.

(Image credit: Wesley Lapointe for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Cuban ambassador says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
item.description: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, a senior Cuban ambassador blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.
item.content:encoded: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, a senior Cuban ambassador blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.
item.dc:creator: Eyder Peralta

item.title: Congress wants to stop corporations from buying up too many single family homes
item.description: It is getting harder and harder to buy a home in the U.S. Congress believes one solution is to put restrictions on corporate ownership of single family homes. Will it really help?
item.content:encoded: It is getting harder and harder to buy a home in the U.S. Congress believes one solution is to put restrictions on corporate ownership of single family homes. Will it really help?
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods
